prompt_preview:
You are a classical planning system. You are given a planning domain and a problem both in PDDL syntax.

STRICT REQUIREMENT: Your response must contain ONLY the executable plan — nothing else. No preamble, no explanation, no "We need to...", no natural-language commentary, no markdown unless it is a

message.content:
(create_traveler_case origin_segment_primary)
(link_policy_rule_to_case origin_segment_primary policy_rule_visa_requirement)
(attach_document_to_case origin_segment_primary document_type_passport)
(validate_case_documents origin_segment_primary document_type_passport)
(flag_entry_requirement origin_segment_primary entry_rule_immigration_clearance document_type_passport)
(assign_reviewer_to_case origin_segment_primary reviewer_role_officer)
(confirm_origin_requirement_by_reviewer origin_segment_primary entry_rule_immigration_clearance reviewer_role_officer)
(release_reviewer_from_case origin_segment_primary reviewer_role_officer)
(detach_document_from_case origin_segment_primary document_type_passport)
(create_traveler_case destination_segment_primary)
(link_policy_rule_to_case destination_segment_primary policy_rule_vaccination_requirement)
(attach_document_to_case destination_segment_primary document_type_passport)
(validate_case_documents destination_segment_primary document_type_passport)
(flag_fare_requirement destination_segment_primary fare_rule_discount_eligibility document_type_passport)
(assign_reviewer_to_case destination_segment_primary reviewer_role_officer)
(confirm_destination_requirement_by_reviewer destination_segment_primary fare_rule_discount_eligibility reviewer_role_officer)
(release_reviewer_from_case destination_segment_primary reviewer_role_officer)
(create_travel_option origin_segment_primary destination_segment_primary entry_rule_immigration_clearance fare_rule_discount_eligibility travel_option_candidate_1)
(attach_document_to_case origin_segment_primary document_type_passport)
(confirm_travel_option_eligibility travel_option_candidate_1 origin_segment_primary document_type_passport)
(mark_origin_segment_travel_ready origin_segment_primary travel_option_candidate_1)
(mark_destination_segment_travel_ready destination_segment_primary travel_option_candidate_1)
(detach_document_from_case origin_segment_primary document_type_passport)
(create_traveler_case policy_exception_request_1)
(link_policy_rule_to_case policy_exception_request_1 policy_rule_fare_eligibility)
(attach_document_to_case policy_exception_request_1 document_type_passport)
(validate_case_documents policy_exception_request_1 document_type_passport)
(reserve_template_bind_to_option policy_exception_request_1 document_template_passport travel_option_candidate_1)
(confirm_template_match_for_request policy_exception_request_1 document_template_passport travel_option_candidate_1 document_type_passport)
(assign_specialist_to_request policy_exception_request_1 specialist_reviewer_medical)
(assign_reviewer_to_case policy_exception_request_1 reviewer_role_officer)
(mark_specialist_review_complete_initial policy_exception_request_1 specialist_reviewer_medical reviewer_role_officer document_template_passport travel_option_candidate_1)
(release_reviewer_from_case policy_exception_request_1 reviewer_role_officer)
(release_specialist_from_request policy_exception_request_1 specialist_reviewer_medical)
(assign_senior_to_request policy_exception_request_1 senior_reviewer_director)
(initiate_senior_review policy_exception_request_1 senior_reviewer_director document_template_passport travel_option_candidate_1)
(release_senior_from_request policy_exception_request_1 senior_reviewer_director)
(finalize_request_no_approver policy_exception_request_1)
(assign_exception_token_to_case policy_exception_request_1 exception_token_temp_allowance document_type_passport)
(apply_exception_token_to_request policy_exception_request_1 policy_rule_fare_eligibility exception_token_temp_allowance)
(detach_document_from_case policy_exception_request_1 document_type_passport)
(create_traveler_case policy_exception_request_2)
(link_policy_rule_to_case policy_exception_request_2 policy_rule_fare_eligibility)
(attach_document_to_case policy_exception_request_2 document_type_passport)
(validate_case_documents policy_exception_request_2 document_type_passport)
(reserve_template_bind_to_option policy_exception_request_2 document_template_visa travel_option_candidate_1)
(confirm_template_match_for_request policy_exception_request_2 document_template_visa travel_option_candidate_1 document_type_passport)
(assign_specialist_to_request policy_exception_request_2 specialist_reviewer_medical)
(assign_reviewer_to_case policy_exception_request_2 reviewer_role_officer)
(mark_specialist_review_complete_initial policy_exception_request_2 specialist_reviewer_medical reviewer_role_officer document_template_visa travel_option_candidate_1)
(release_reviewer_from_case policy_exception_request_2 reviewer_role_officer)
(release_specialist_from_request policy_exception_request_2 specialist_reviewer_medical)
(assign_senior_to_request policy_exception_request_2 senior_reviewer_director)
(initiate_senior_review policy_exception_request_2 senior_reviewer_director document_template_visa travel_option_candidate_1)
(release_senior_from_request policy_exception_request_2 senior_reviewer_director)
(finalize_request_no_approver policy_exception_request_2)
(assign_exception_token_to_case policy_exception_request_2 exception_token_temp_allowance document_type_passport)
(apply_exception_token_to_request policy_exception_request_2 policy_rule_fare_eligibility exception_token_temp_allowance)
(detach_document_from_case policy_exception_request_2 document_type_passport)
(attach_document_to_case origin_segment_primary document_type_passport)
(assign_exception_token_to_case origin_segment_primary exception_token_temp_allowance document_type_passport)
(apply_exception_token_to_origin_segment origin_segment_primary policy_rule_visa_requirement exception_token_temp_allowance)
(detach_document_from_case origin_segment_primary document_type_passport)
(attach_document_to_case destination_segment_primary document_type_passport)
(assign_exception_token_to_case destination_segment_primary exception_token_temp_allowance document_type_passport)
(apply_exception_token_to_destination_segment destination_segment_primary policy_rule_vaccination_requirement exception_token_temp_allowance)
message.reasoning:
The problem is complex, but I need to find a plan to achieve the goal: make origin_segment_primary, destination_segment_primary, policy_exception_request_1, and policy_exception_request_2 all ready_for_travel.


## Planning the Approach  
- The goal is to make origin_segment_primary, destination_segment_primary, policy_exception_request_1, and policy_exception_request_2 all ready_for_travel.  
- The problem involves coordinating multiple components, suggesting a step-by-step strategy to ensure readiness for each.

## Examining Entity Relationships  
- Origin_segment and destination_segment are typed as travel_segment, a subtype of resource_group, not traveler_case, while policy_exception_request is traveler_case.  
- This suggests a modeling distinction where segments are treated as resources, but may need to be handled as cases for certain actions like ready_for_travel.  

## Investigating Actions for Readiness  
- Actions like apply_exception_token_to_origin_segment, apply_exception_token_to_destination_segment, and apply_exception_token_to_request set ready_for_travel, requiring case_exception_token_assigned_flag, a linked policy_rule, and an available exception_token.  
- Assigning an exception_token requires approval_granted_flag, a linked document, and an available token, with effects including setting the assigned flag and linking the token.  

## Checking Approval Processes  
- Approval_granted_flag is set by actions like finalize_request_no_approver or mark_origin_segment_travel_ready, with segment actions also requiring origin/dest_segment_requirement_satisfied, assembled travel_option, and confirmed eligibility.  
- For segments to assign tokens, they likely need to meet these readiness conditions first, aligning with the goal of ready_for_travel.  

## Analyzing Case Registration  
- No create_traveler_case action has been called yet, and initial conditions show nothing registered, suggesting segments and policy_exception_requests need case creation for readiness.  
- Segments, however, are not typed as traveler_case, indicating potential issues in applying traveler_case actions to them without further modeling.

## Exploring Document Management  
- Only one document, a passport, is available for use across cases, limiting validation options.  
- Cases can attach, validate, and detach documents sequentially, as detach_document_from_case makes the document available again after use.  

## Assessing Escalation Categories  
- Escalation category medical is available, but operational is linked to policy_exception_request_2 without being marked as available in initial conditions.  
- This discrepancy suggests operational escalation may need categorization or resolution for request processing.  

## Reviewing Travel Option Assembly  
- Travel_option assembly requires checked segments, flagged/supported rules, and satisfied conditions, which depend on document validation.  
- Segments likely need processing first to enable travel_option readiness, aligning with approval_granted_flag requirements.

## Building Case Processes  
- Creating a case involves linking a policy_rule and attaching the passport document, which can be reused via detachment.  
- Validation of documents, such as attaching evidence for origin and destination rules, consumes evidence temporarily but supports readiness.  

## Assembling Travel Options  
- Travel options can be created with both origin and destination supports, requiring checked segments and supported rules, setting them as assembled and eligible.  
- Eligibility confirmation needs the passport document, ensuring segments are confirmed and ready for further processing.  

## Managing Segment Readiness  
- Segments become satisfied through processing evidence, such as process_origin_evidence, which requires validated documents and supported rules, then sets satisfaction and flags rules.  
- After both origin and destination are satisfied, segments can be marked ready for travel, granting approval and making evidence available again.  

## Handling Request Escalation  
- Requests can achieve approval via escalation, requiring validated requests, available categories, and document attachment, leading to categorization.  
- The escalation review path includes stages like first and second reviews, finalizing with approval after both stages, leveraging available senior reviewers and approvers.

## Managing Document Movement  
- Only one passport document is available, requiring it to be moved between origin and destination segments for processing.  
- Detaching the document from a segment makes it available for attachment elsewhere, but conflicts arise when processes like evidence processing require the document to remain attached.  

## Sequencing Case Preparation  
- The sequence involves creating traveler cases for origin and destination, linking policy rules, and attaching the passport document, but rules and the document must be reused due to limited availability.  
- Policy rules like visa_requirement and fare_rule_discount_eligibility are linked to cases, becoming unavailable until later steps like applying tokens release them for reuse.  

## Handling Token and Rule Reuse  
- The exception_token_temp_allowance can be assigned to a segment, applied, and then released for reuse with the next segment, ensuring efficient token management.  
- After applying a token, linked policy rules become available again, allowing sequential processing across multiple cases without additional resources.

## Processing Request Approvals  
- After origin and destination segments receive approval, focus shifts to processing requests, starting with linking the available policy_rule_fare_eligibility to the first request.  
- The second request faces a challenge as escalation_category_operational is unavailable, limiting categorization options and requiring alternative paths like specialist or senior review.  

## Exploring Alternative Review Paths  
- Specialist and senior review paths offer solutions for requests, requiring actions like mark_specialist_review_complete_initial, which needs request_template_matched and specialist assignment.  
- These paths involve linking templates to travel options, ensuring conditions like not_dest_eligible are met, and setting flags like specialist_assigned_flag for progression.

## Assigning Specialist and Senior Reviewers  
- Assigning a specialist to a request requires validating case documents and ensures the specialist, like medical, is available; only one specialist can be assigned at a time but can be released for reuse.  
- Senior reviewers can be assigned after specialist review, requiring validated requests and availability, enabling progression through review stages without needing escalation.  

## Ensuring Template and Option Readiness  
- Reserving a template for a request needs a validated request, linked option, and confirmed eligibility, with templates like passport and visa available for separate requests.  
- The process sets the template as reserved and bound to the option, ensuring conditions like not_dest_eligible are met for specialist review completion.  

## Managing Policy Rule and Token Allocation  
- One remaining policy rule, fare_eligibility, can be used sequentially for requests, released after token application to enable reuse for the next request.  
- Exception tokens, shared across requests, are assigned post-approval, ensuring readiness for travel while managing limited resources efficiently.

## Preparing Travel Segments  
- Attaching passport document to origin segment and confirming eligibility for travel option candidate 1 sets the stage for readiness.  
- Marking both origin and destination segments as travel-ready ensures approval_granted_flag is set, allowing progression to request handling.  

## Handling Document Transfers  
- Detaching the passport document from the origin segment is necessary before attaching it to policy_exception_request_1 for further processing.  
- Validating case documents for the request ensures readiness for template reservation and specialist review.  

## Reserving Templates for Requests  
- Reserving the passport template for policy_exception_request_1 requires confirmed eligibility, assembled status, and validated request, binding it to travel option candidate 1.  
- Confirming the template match ensures the request has the necessary documentation, like the passport, for progression.  

## Assigning Reviewers and Completing Reviews  
- Assigning a medical specialist and an officer reviewer to policy_exception_request_1 requires validated documents, setting flags for review completion.  
- Initiating senior review and finalizing the request without an approver completes the process, granting approval for req1.

## Reviewing Senior Review Process  
- Assigning a senior director for req1's review involves initiating a senior review with the passport template and travel option.  
- Releasing the senior director from req1 is possible after review, ensuring resources are freed for other requests like req2.  

## Finalizing Requests  
- Finalizing req1 without an approver requires completing the senior review, setting approval_granted_flag.  
- Assigning and applying fare policy tokens to req1 needs the passport document attached, confirmed after template matching.

pddl2nl_prompt.note: autoplanbench_pddl2nl: run_save_descriptions.py --type full — шаблоны/JSON, без вызова LLM на построение промпта для сэмпла.

domain_setup.note: Файл domain_llm_usage_seedN.json не найден для section/subsection этого id (старый autoplanbench или домен не пересобирали). После run_domain_setup с обновлённым autoplanbench файл появится рядом с domain_description_seedN.json.

pipeline_usage.totals_scope_note: Per-run totals = planning + pddl2nl_prompt + nl2pddl (только tracked=true). domain_setup всегда вне totals (included_in_per_run_totals=false); цифры в блоке — для справки, агрегировать отдельно: domain_llm_usage_seed*.json + дедуп в collect_pipeline_token_usage.py.
